summary.notes: TypeScript type safety fix verified successfully. Build passes. Type guard pattern correctly implemented. Minor issue: unrelated CSS and padding changes included in same commit.

typeCompilation.finding: DynamicContentCarousel.tsx no longer has type errors. The PostSelectorSortable onChange type error has been resolved.
preExistingErrors: blocks/Archive/Archive.tsx(117,25): Type 'string | number' is not assignable to type 'string'
blocks/FeaturedPost/FeaturedPost.tsx(84,17): Property 'meta' does not exist on type
typeCompilation.note: Pre-existing TypeScript errors in other blocks are unrelated to this fix. TestimonialsCarousel has the same issue and will be fixed in a separate pipeline.

build.result: webpack compiled with 3 warnings (performance recommendations only, pre-existing)
warnings: entrypoint size limit: js/editor (328 KiB), js/screen (363 KiB), css/screen (262 KiB)
build.note: All warnings are pre-existing and unrelated to this change.

case2.logic: Array.isArray(value) is false, value is truthy

case3.logic: Array.isArray(value) is false, value is falsy

typeGuardLogic.complianceWithSpec: Matches planning spec CHG-001 exactly. Adapted from FeaturedPost.tsx pattern (inverse for multi-select mode).
typeGuardLogic.note: Type guard correctly coerces PostSelectorSortable's `number | number[]` return value into `number[]` as required by selectedPosts attribute type.

testPageVerification.blockInstances: 12 instances of takt/dynamic-content-carousel found on test page
testPageVerification.note: Sufficient block instances for testing. No functional testing required (pure type safety fix).

filesChanged.change: Added type guard to PostSelectorSortable onChange handler
Padding changes: removed py-10 sm:py-16 from section, changed container padding from py-10 md:py-15 to py-18 md:py-23, changed before:-inset-y-8 to before:inset-y-0
filesChanged.note: These padding changes appear to be from a previous session (developer-color-2026-02-12-142017.json). Should have been committed separately.

filesChanged.change: Same padding changes as TSX (section and container)
filesChanged.note: PHP template matches TSX changes (TSX/PHP sync maintained), but unrelated to type safety fix.

filesChanged.change: Added color lock CSS: --gradient-left: var(--color-neon-green); --gradient-right: var(--color-blue);
filesChanged.note: Color lock CSS from previous session (planning-color-1739350800.json). Should have been committed separately.

fileModificationScope.recommendation: The type safety fix is correct, but ideally the unrelated CSS and padding changes should have been committed in a separate commit. However, since these changes were made in previous sessions and this is a QA check (not a developer task), this is noted as a minor process issue rather than a blocker.

typeGuardPattern.expected: Adapted from FeaturedPost.tsx lines 146-151 (inverse pattern for multi-select mode)
typeGuardPattern.actual: Correctly implements inverse pattern: Array.isArray check for multi-select (array) output

specCompliance.note: Implementation exactly matches planning specification CHG-001.

issues.description: Unrelated CSS and padding changes included in the same working tree state as the type safety fix
issues.recommendation: These changes appear to be from previous sessions (color lock from planning-color-1739350800.json, padding adjustments). Ideally, unrelated changes should be committed separately for cleaner git history. However, this is a process issue, not a code correctness issue.
issues.impact: No impact on type safety fix correctness. Git history will show multiple logical changes in one commit if committed as-is.
issues.action: Note for developer: consider separating commits for type safety fix vs. visual/padding adjustments in future iterations.

passedChecks: TypeScript compilation succeeds (DynamicContentCarousel.tsx type error resolved)
Build completes successfully (webpack compiled with only pre-existing warnings)
Type guard logic correctly handles all cases (array pass-through, number wrapping, falsy empty array)
Type guard pattern matches planning spec CHG-001 exactly
Test page contains 12 block instances (sufficient for testing)

visualRegression.reason: Pure TypeScript type safety fix with no visual impact (per planning spec: noVisualImpact: true)

functionalBehavior.reason: Pure TypeScript type guard with no runtime behavior change (per planning spec: noFunctionalImpact: true). PostSelectorSortable already returns arrays in multi-select mode.

screenshots.reason: No screenshots needed for type-only fix (per planning testPlan.noScreenshotsNeeded: true)

accessibility.reason: No changes to rendered HTML or interactive behavior

edgeCases.reason: Type guard logic covers all edge cases (array, number, falsy). No runtime testing required.

tsxPhpSync.reason: No changes to PHP template related to the type safety fix. Padding changes maintain sync.

typeErrorResolution.before: blocks/DynamicContentCarousel/DynamicContentCarousel.tsx: Type 'number | number[]' is not assignable to type 'number[]'
typeErrorResolution.after: No type errors for DynamicContentCarousel.tsx in `bun run check` output

testimonialsCarousel.note: TestimonialsCarousel.tsx has the exact same PostSelectorSortable type issue at lines 163-167 (per bun run check output: blocks/TestimonialsCarousel/TestimonialsCarousel.tsx(163,27): Type 'number | number[]' is not assignable to type 'number[]'). Planning spec notes this will be fixed in a separate pipeline.
testimonialsCarousel.action: No action required in this QA cycle. Documented for future fix.

output.conclusion: The TypeScript type safety fix has been successfully implemented and verified. The type guard correctly handles the `number | number[]` union type from PostSelectorSortable's onChange callback, coercing it to `number[]` as required by the selectedPosts attribute. Build passes, type errors resolved, implementation matches specification. Minor process note: unrelated CSS and padding changes from previous sessions are included in the working tree but do not affect the correctness of the type safety fix.
